title: syntax-rules: a pattern variable inside an unquote ,x within a quasiquote template is NOT substituted (treated as free/undefined)
goal: Found by the P6e metaprogramming depth harness (2026-07-03). When a syntax-rules template contains a quasiquote with an unquoted pattern variable, e.g. (car `(,(+ x 1))) or (list `(a ,x)), the pattern variable x is NOT hygiene-substituted during expansion -- it is left as a free variable and the compiler reports 'Undefined variable: x', silently evaluating it as 0. So (q2 5) with ((_ x) (list `(a ,x))) yields ((a ())) instead of ((a 5)); (add1q 0) with ((_ x) (car `(,(+ x 1)))) yields 1 only because x collapses to 0 (0+1). The macro expander does not walk into quasiquote/unquote subforms to substitute pattern variables. qq_macro family in the harness: max-correct-depth 0 (d1 passes only coincidentally).
acceptance: (define-syntax add1q (syntax-rules () ((_ x) (car `(,(+ x 1)))))) (add1q 0) => 1 with x bound (not a free-var error)
no 'Undefined variable' for pattern vars used under quasiquote/unquote in a template
campaign_payoff: Quasiquote in macro templates is the standard way to build code; dropping pattern-var substitution there produces silently wrong expansions.
repro:
printf "(require stdlib)
(define-syntax q2 (syntax-rules () ((_ x) (list \x60(a ,x)))))
(display (q2 5))(newline)
" > /tmp/t.esk; build/eshkol-run -r /tmp/t.esk  # prints ((a ())) and 'Undefined variable: x'
resolution: RESOLVED 2026-07-04 (branch fix/qq-macro-depth): MacroExpander::substituteBindings and substituteBindingsAtIndex now recurse into ESHKOL_QUASIQUOTE_OP / UNQUOTE_OP / UNQUOTE_SPLICING_OP / QUOTE_OP (they share the call_op layout), so pattern variables under a quasiquote/unquote in a template are substituted. expandNode also descends into quasiquote/unquote (not quote) so macro calls introduced inside an unquote escape get re-expanded. qq_macro family: MCD 1 -> 32 (both r+aot). Verified (add1q 0)=1..depth4=4, (q 5) with ((_ x)(list `(a ,x)))=>((a 5)).
